result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro [command]

IO.Astrodynamics.CLI

Commands:
  ephemeris                                    Compute ephemeris of given object
  sub-point                                    Compute sub observer point of given object in planetodetic, planetocentric or planetocentric cartesian coordinates
  angular-separation                           Compute angular separation between given objects
  orientation                                  Compute orientations of given object
  find-windows-from-coordinate-constraint      Find time windows when constraint occurs
  find-windows-from-distance-constraint        Find time windows when constraint occurs
  find-windows-from-occultation-constraint     Find time windows when constraint occurs
  find-windows-from-fov-constraint             Find time windows when a target is in field of view of given instrument
  find-windows-from-illumination-constraint    Find time windows when constraint occurs
  orbital-parameters-converter                 Convert an orbital parameters to another type at given epoch in given frame
  frame-converter                              Convert a frame to another at given epoch
  time-converter                               Convert a time system to another
  celestial-body-info                          Get celestial body informations
  propagate                                    Propagate a small body
  --version                                    

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help message
  --version     Show version

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro angular-separation [--help] kernels-path observer-id first-object-id second-object-id epoch aberration

Compute angular separation between given objects

Arguments:
  0: kernels-path        Kernels directory path (Required)
  1: observer-id         Observer identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  2: first-object-id     First object identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  3: second-object-id    Second object identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  4: epoch               Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  5: aberration          Aberration (Default: None)

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help message

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro ephemeris [--to-state-vector=<true|false>] [--to-keplerian] [--to-equinoctial] [--help] kernels-path object-id observer-id begin end step frame aberration

Compute ephemeris of given object

Arguments:
  0: kernels-path    Kernels directory path (Required)
  1: object-id       Object identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  2: observer-id     Observer identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  3: begin           Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  4: end             Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  5: step            Step size <d.hh:mm:ss.fff> (Required)
  6: frame           Frame (Default: ICRF)
  7: aberration      Aberration (Default: None)

Options:
  -s, --to-state-vector=<true|false>    Display result as state vector (Default: True)
  -k, --to-keplerian                    Display result as Keplerian elements
  -q, --to-equinoctial                  Display result as equinoctial elements
  -h, --help                            Show help message

Arguments:
  0: kernels-path    Kernels directory path (Required)

					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  4: end             Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  5: step            Step size <d.hh:mm:ss.fff> (Required)
  6: frame           Frame (Default: ICRF)
  7: aberration      Aberration (Default: None)

Options:
  -s, --to-state-vector=<true|false>    Display result as state vector (Default: True)
  -k, --to-keplerian                    Display result as Keplerian elements
  -q, --to-equinoctial                  Display result as equinoctial elements
  -h, --help                            Show help message

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro find-windows-from-coordinate-constraint [--help] kernels-path observer-id target-id coordinate-system coordinate relational-operator value begin end step aberration adjust-value frame

Find time windows when constraint occurs

Arguments:
  0: kernels-path           Kernels directory path (Required)
  1: observer-id            Observer identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  2: target-id              Target identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  3: coordinate-system      CoordinateSystem (RECTANGULAR, LATITUDINAL, RA/DEC, SPHERICAL, CYLINDRICAL, GEODETIC, PLANETOGRAPHIC) (Required)
  4: coordinate             Coordinate (X,Y,Z,ALTITUDE,COLATITUDE,DECLINATION,LATITUDE,LONGITUDE,RADIUS,RANGE,RIGHT ASCENSION) (Required)
  5: relational-operator    Relational operator used to define search constraint (<,>,=,ABSMIN,ABSMAX,LOCMIN,LOCMAX) (Required)
  6: value                  Value used by operator (Required)
  7: begin                  Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  8: end                    Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  9: step                   Initial step size <d.hh:mm:ss.fff> (Required)
  10: aberration            Aberration (Default: None)
  11: adjust-value          Adjust value (Used by ABSMIN and ABSMAX only. Return window when value is between ABSMIN and ABSMIN + adjust and ABSMAX - adjust and ABSMAX) (Default: 0)
  12: frame                 Frame (Default: ICRF)

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help message

IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro find-windows-from-illumination-constraint [--help] kernels-path observer-id target-id illumination-id fixed-frame planetodetic illumination-type relational-operator value begin end step aberration adjust-value

Arguments:

  1: observer-id            Observer identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  2: target-id              Target identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  3: illumination-id        Illumination source identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  4: fixed-frame            Target frame (Required)
  5: planetodetic           Planetodetic coordinates <longitude latitude altitude> (Required)
  6: illumination-type      Illumination type - Phase or Incidence or Emission (Required)
  7: relational-operator    Relational operator (<,>,=,ABSMIN,ABSMAX,LOCMIN,LOCMAX) (Required)
  8: value                  Value (Required)
  9: begin                  Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>

  10: end                   Epoch that matches the following format :

  12: aberration            Aberration (Default: None)
  13: adjust-value          Adjust value (Used by ABSMIN and ABSMAX. Return window when value is between ABSMIN and ABSMIN + adjust and ABSMAX - adjust and ABSMAX) (Default: 0)

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help message

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental Usage: astro orbital-parameters-converter [--from-state-vector] [--from-keplerian] [--from-equinoctial] [--from-tle] [--to-state-vector] [--to-keplerian] [--to-equinoctial] [--help] kernels-path center-of-motion-id orbital-parameters-values orbital-parameters-epoch frame epoch target-frame Convert an orbital parameters to another type at given epoch in given frame Arguments: 0: kernels-path Directory kernels path (Required) 1: center-of-motion-id Center of motion identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required) 2: orbital-parameters-values Orbital parameters values like state vector, keplerian, equinoctial or two lines.

(Required) 3: orbital-parameters-epoch Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 4: frame Frame (Required) 5: epoch Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 6: target-frame Target frame (Default: ICRF) Options: -s, --from-state-vector Orbital parameters values represents a state vector -k, --from-keplerian Orbital parameters values represents keplerian elements -q, --from-equinoctial Orbital parameters values represents equinoctial elements -t, --from-tle Orbital parameters values represents two lines elements --to-state-vector Convert to state vector --to-keplerian Convert to keplerian elements --to-equinoctial Convert to Equinoctial -h, --help Show help message

IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental Usage: astro orbital-parameters-converter [--from-state-vector] [--from-keplerian] [--from-equinoctial] [--from-tle] [--to-state-vector] [--to-keplerian] [--to-equinoctial] [--help] kernels-path center-of-motion-id orbital-parameters-values orbital-parameters-epoch frame epoch target-frame Convert an orbital parameters to another type at given epoch in given frame Arguments: 0: kernels-path Directory kernels path (Required) 1: center-of-motion-id Center of motion identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required) 2: orbital-parameters-values Orbital parameters values like state vector, keplerian, equinoctial or two lines.

(Required) 3: orbital-parameters-epoch Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 4: frame Frame (Required) 5: epoch Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 6: target-frame Target frame (Default: ICRF) Options: -s, --from-state-vector Orbital parameters values represents a state vector -k, --from-keplerian Orbital parameters values represents keplerian elements -q, --from-equinoctial Orbital parameters values represents equinoctial elements -t, --from-tle Orbital parameters values represents two lines elements --to-state-vector Convert to state vector --to-keplerian Convert to keplerian elements --to-equinoctial Convert to Equinoctial -h, --help Show help message

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro orientation [--help] kernels-path object-id begin end step frame

Compute orientations of given object

Arguments:
  0: kernels-path    Kernels directory path (Required)
  1: object-id       Object identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  2: begin           Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  3: end             Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  4: step            Step size <d.hh:mm:ss.fff> (Required)
  5: frame           Frame (Default: ICRF)

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help message

					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental Usage: astro propagate [--from-state-vector] [--from-keplerian] [--from-equinoctial] [--from-tle] [--use-solar-radiation-pressure] [--use-atmospheric-drag] [--earth-geopotential-degree ] [--help] kernels-path body-name center-of-motion-id orbital-parameters-values orbital-parameters-epoch frame begin end output-directory body-mass area cd celestial-bodies0 ... celestial-bodiesN Propagate a small body Arguments: 0: kernels-path Kernels directory path (Required) 1: body-name Body name (Required) 2: center-of-motion-id Center of motion identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required) 3: orbital-parameters-values Orbital parameters values like state vector, keplerian, equinoctial or two lines.

(Required) 4: orbital-parameters-epoch Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 5: frame Frame (Required) 6: begin Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 7: end Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 8: output-directory Output directory (Required) 9: body-mass Body mass (Default: 100) 10: area Body projected area (Default: 1) 11: cd Drag coefficient (Default: 0.3) 12: celestial-bodies Celestial bodies involved into the propagation in addition to the center of motion Options: -s, --from-state-vector Orbital parameters values represents a state vector -k, --from-keplerian Orbital parameters values represents keplerian elements -q, --from-equinoctial Orbital parameters values represents equinoctial elements -t, --from-tle Orbital parameters values represents two lines elements -r, --use-solar-radiation-pressure Include solar radiation pressure perturbation -a, --use-atmospheric-drag Include atmospheric drag perturbation (Earth and Mars only) -n, --earth-geopotential-degree Number of degrees used by earth geopotential model (Max. 100 (Default: 10) -h, --help Show help message

commands.payload:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental Usage: astro propagate [--from-state-vector] [--from-keplerian] [--from-equinoctial] [--from-tle] [--use-solar-radiation-pressure] [--use-atmospheric-drag] [--earth-geopotential-degree ] [--help] kernels-path body-name center-of-motion-id orbital-parameters-values orbital-parameters-epoch frame begin end output-directory body-mass area cd celestial-bodies0 ... celestial-bodiesN Propagate a small body Arguments: 0: kernels-path Kernels directory path (Required) 1: body-name Body name (Required) 2: center-of-motion-id Center of motion identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required) 3: orbital-parameters-values Orbital parameters values like state vector, keplerian, equinoctial or two lines.

(Required) 4: orbital-parameters-epoch Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 5: frame Frame (Required) 6: begin Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 7: end Epoch that matches the following format : Time format (ISO 8601) 2024-01-01T21:00:00Z Seconds from J2000 (decimal value) 144521.0 Julian date (decimal value) 245000.0 JD (Required) 8: output-directory Output directory (Required) 9: body-mass Body mass (Default: 100) 10: area Body projected area (Default: 1) 11: cd Drag coefficient (Default: 0.3) 12: celestial-bodies Celestial bodies involved into the propagation in addition to the center of motion Options: -s, --from-state-vector Orbital parameters values represents a state vector -k, --from-keplerian Orbital parameters values represents keplerian elements -q, --from-equinoctial Orbital parameters values represents equinoctial elements -t, --from-tle Orbital parameters values represents two lines elements -r, --use-solar-radiation-pressure Include solar radiation pressure perturbation -a, --use-atmospheric-drag Include atmospheric drag perturbation (Earth and Mars only) -n, --earth-geopotential-degree Number of degrees used by earth geopotential model (Max. 100 (Default: 10) -h, --help Show help message

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro sub-point [--planetodetic] [--cartesian] [--help] kernels-path object-id celestial-body-id epoch aberration

Compute sub observer point of given object in planetodetic, planetocentric or planetocentric cartesian coordinates

Arguments:
  0: kernels-path         Kernels directory path (Required)
  1: object-id            Object identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  2: celestial-body-id    Identifier of the celestial body onto which the object is projected (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  3: epoch                Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  4: aberration           Aberration (Default: None)

Options:
  -d, --planetodetic    Display result as planetodetic coordinates
  -c, --cartesian       Display result as planetocentric cartesian coordinates
  -h, --help            Show help message

commands.payload:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro sub-point [--planetodetic] [--cartesian] [--help] kernels-path object-id celestial-body-id epoch aberration

Compute sub observer point of given object in planetodetic, planetocentric or planetocentric cartesian coordinates

Arguments:
  0: kernels-path         Kernels directory path (Required)
  1: object-id            Object identifier (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  2: celestial-body-id    Identifier of the celestial body onto which the object is projected (Naif Identifier) (Required)
  3: epoch                Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)
  4: aberration           Aberration (Default: None)

Options:
  -d, --planetodetic    Display result as planetodetic coordinates
  -c, --cartesian       Display result as planetocentric cartesian coordinates
  -h, --help            Show help message

result.stdout:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro time-converter [--to-tdb] [--to-utc] [--to-local] [--to-julian] [--to-seconds-from-j2000] [--to-date-time] [--help] epoch

Convert a time system to another

Arguments:
  0: epoch    Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)

Options:
  -t, --to-tdb                   Convert to TDB
  -u, --to-utc                   Convert to UTC
  -l, --to-local                 Convert to Local
  -j, --to-julian                Convert to Julian date
  -e, --to-seconds-from-j2000    Convert to elapsed seconds from J2000 epoch
  -d, --to-date-time             Convert to Time (ISO 8601)
  -h, --help                     Show help message

commands.payload:
IO.Astrodynamics.CLI v0.0.1.0 experimental
Usage: astro time-converter [--to-tdb] [--to-utc] [--to-local] [--to-julian] [--to-seconds-from-j2000] [--to-date-time] [--help] epoch

Convert a time system to another

Arguments:
  0: epoch    Epoch that matches the following format :
					Time format (ISO 8601)			2024-01-01T21:00:00Z
					Seconds from J2000 (decimal value)		144521.0 <UTC or TDB>
					Julian date (decimal value)			245000.0 JD<UTC or TDB> (Required)

Options:
  -t, --to-tdb                   Convert to TDB
  -u, --to-utc                   Convert to UTC
  -l, --to-local                 Convert to Local
  -j, --to-julian                Convert to Julian date
  -e, --to-seconds-from-j2000    Convert to elapsed seconds from J2000 epoch
  -d, --to-date-time             Convert to Time (ISO 8601)
  -h, --help                     Show help message

staticCommands.name: angular-separation

staticCommands.name: find-windows-from-coordinate-constraint
staticCommands.description: Find time windows when constraint occurs

values.name: coordinateSystem
values.description: CoordinateSystem (RECTANGULAR, LATITUDINAL, RA/DEC, SPHERICAL, CYLINDRICAL, GEODETIC, PLANETOGRAPHIC)

values.name: relationalOperator
values.description: Relational operator used to define search constraint (<,>,=,ABSMIN,ABSMAX,LOCMIN,LOCMAX)

values.name: adjustValue
values.description: Adjust value (Used by ABSMIN and ABSMAX only. Return window when value is between ABSMIN and ABSMIN + adjust and ABSMAX - adjust and ABSMAX)

values.name: adjustValue
